PP.sub: Spot squeeze pushes homo sharply higher
bullets: Domestic PP offers kept climbing, led by homo grades, as sellers ration spot volume and some report running out of stock entirely.
Distributors are diverting NSRP yarn/injection grades to Indonesian export at rising USD prices, tightening the volume left for the local market.
China-linked import offers (PetroChina, Jinneng) rose 30–45 USD/ton on the week as Chinese domestic prices stay firm enough to reopen SE Asia-to-China arbitrage.

PE.sub: Import offers keep climbing, LLDPE leads the domestic move
bullets: Chinese CIF offers for LLDPE and HDPE film/blow grades rose again this week, with one distributor’s film/blow list up sharply versus last week.
Domestic LLDPE film jumped 2,000–3,000 VND/kg d/d as trader inventories thinned; some northern traders were forced to buy back stock to cover orders.
HDPE and LDPE offers were comparatively steadier, up only a few hundred VND/kg, as buyers stay cautious and import mostly on a hand-to-mouth basis.

PVC.sub: Producers nudge prices up on freight, not demand
bullets: AGC Group’s August offers from Thailand (Vinythai) and Indonesia (Asahimas) fell 30–35 USD/ton m/m, keeping oil-based CIF offers in a 690–720 USD/ton band.
Domestically, ACVN held its August list flat versus late June after briefly testing a lower quote to loyal customers last week.
Sources say the modest recovery mainly reflects higher freight rates and the broader resin uptrend rather than any pickup in end-demand.

editorial.changedNote: 23 of 54 tracked grades moved vs. the previous session.

regional.l: PE – HDPE Blow 5502S (multi-supplier, China) CIF Hai Phong
PE – HDPE Film FS7000 (STL, China) CIF
PP – PP Yarn L5E89 (PetroChina, China) CIF
PP – PP Yarn/Injection (Jinneng, China) CIF Hai Phong

summary: Domestic PP offers, especially homo grades, rose sharply this week as sellers hold back spot volume and prioritize export shipments.
NSRP yarn/injection cargoes are being redirected to Indonesia at higher USD prices, tightening supply available to the domestic market.
Chinese import offers (PetroChina, Jinneng) climbed 30–45 USD/ton, with arbitrage back open from Southeast Asia into China.
paras: Domestic PP offer prices kept climbing this week, with the PP homo line rising sharply — roughly 2,000 VND/kg above late last week’s levels. Some sellers are offering only limited quantities, a few report running out of spot goods altogether, and some large distributors are prioritizing export shipments to capture better margins.

On NSRP’s PP yarn/injection 114G and 105G, several large distributors exported successfully to Indonesia last week at 1,130–1,150 USD/ton CIF; this week some raised their Indonesia export offers to 1,300 USD/ton CIF, with a few going higher still. That has left spot PP supply noticeably tighter at home.

In the north, settled prices for NSRP’s PP 114G delivered by container jumped to 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, with small-volume, warehouse-delivered offers at 33,500–34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. BSR’s PP yarn T3034 moved from 33,000–33,500 VND/kg incl. VAT to 33,500–34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

In the south, the general PP homo offer level rose to 33,000–34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT after an increase the day before, though buyer sentiment remains cautious — sources suggest sellers may partly be testing the market rather than reflecting firm demand. Replenishment supply is expected to normalize in early August, which should cap how long the elevated prices hold.

On imports, PetroChina’s PP yarn L5E89 was offered at 1,160–1,170 USD/ton CIF (CIF HCMC runs about 10 USD/ton above CIF Hai Phong), up 45 USD/ton on the week. Jinneng’s PP yarn/injection was offered at 1,180 USD/ton CIF Hai Phong, up 30 USD/ton, as firm Chinese domestic prices reopen arbitrage back into China.

summary: Chinese CIF offers for LLDPE and HDPE grades continued rising, with one distributor’s list up sharply on the week.
Domestic LLDPE film jumped 2,000–3,000 VND/kg d/d as trader inventories thinned; some had to buy back stock to cover orders.
HDPE and LDPE moved up only modestly, with buyers still importing cautiously on a hand-to-mouth basis.
paras: Import offers for PE from Chinese suppliers to Vietnam continued to rise this week. As of 21/7, PetroChina offered LLDPE Film at 1,070–1,080 USD/ton CIF, up a slight 15 USD/ton from late last week for August delivery, though the supplier flagged that deals still depend on final confirmation.

A large distributor offering HDPE blow grades sourced from China and the US raised prices 5–15 USD/ton on the week: HDPE blow 5502S (Yulong, Baofeng, Sinopec, Huajin) at 1,200–1,250 USD/ton CIF Hai Phong, and US-origin Marlex 5502BN at 1,242 USD/ton CIF. STL’s HDPE Film FS7000 rose 15 USD/ton to 1,195–1,205 USD/ton CIF.

A distributor for China’s ZPC offered LLDPE Film/injection at 1,245–1,265 USD/ton CIF, LLDPE roto at 1,325, LDPE Film at 1,445, HDPE Film at 1,225, and HDPE blow at 1,275 USD/ton CIF — all up very sharply on the week. Separately, US-origin Montachem LLDPE Film was offered at 990 USD/ton CIF HCMC, but only for distant Oct–Nov delivery.

Domestically, LLDPE led the move: some northern traders had to buy back LLDPE Film from other trading companies at 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT to fill orders, while most sellers raised offers to 34,000–36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, up 2,000–3,000 VND/kg from late last week.

HDPE film/yarn/blow/injection was comparatively steady at 35,000–37,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, up only 500–1,000 VND/kg. LSP’s distribution office, for example, settled HDPE blow 5502 (CP Chem, Singapore) at 35,300 VND/kg incl. VAT this week versus 33,500 VND/kg incl. VAT last week, though buyer bids trailed the settled price by 500–600 VND/kg.

LDPE was similarly flat to up a slight 500–1,000 VND/kg. Grades with thin domestic spot inventory — Lotte Titan’s 260GG and PTT’s 2426K — were offered up to 41,000–42,000 VND/kg incl. VAT in the north. Traders continue pushing offers higher while buyers stay cautious, importing only small volumes and buying raw materials hand-to-mouth.

summary: AGC Group’s August CIF offers from Thailand and Indonesia fell 30–35 USD/ton on the month.
ACVN held its domestic August list flat versus late June after briefly testing a lower quote last week.
The modest uptick in commercial prices mainly reflects freight costs and the broader resin trend, not stronger demand.
paras: AGC Group offered PVC for August 2026 shipment to Vietnam on 21/7: Thailand’s Vinythai (AVT) K66 at 700 USD/ton CIF and K57 at 710 USD/ton CIF, both down 30–40 USD/ton from the previous month. Indonesia’s Asahimas (ASC) K66 was offered at 695 USD/ton CIF for large customers and 705 USD/ton CIF for others, down 35 USD/ton.

A day earlier, a large distributor offered imported PVC S1000/K67 from China’s Sinopec at 695–700 USD/ton CIF Hai Phong and 700–705 USD/ton CIF HCMC for spot July delivery; most buyers reportedly bid about 20 USD/ton below that list. A distributor for Thailand’s SCG reported August settled sales of PVC SP660/SG660 at 710–720 USD/ton CIF, though few buyers have committed at what they see as a still-high price.
Taken together, oil-based PVC offers for August shipment from large Chinese and Southeast Asian distributors to Vietnam range 690–720 USD/ton CIF.

Domestically, producer AGC Vina (ACVN) priced its August PVC PM66R at 19,700/19,900/20,000 VND/kg excl. VAT depending on customer and volume — flat versus the late-June list. Last week ACVN had briefly quoted loyal customers 18,800 VND/kg excl. VAT before quickly pulling that offer, suggesting prices have recovered slightly since.

Sources say Middle East tensions between the US and Iran have had little direct effect on PVC; the pause in declines, or slight recovery, mainly follows the broader resin uptrend and a modest lift from freight rates. In the commercial market only scattered sellers nudged prices up, and coal-based PVC ticked up 100–200 VND/kg on higher China freight; overall PVC offer and transaction prices commonly range 20,000–22,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.
